strategy_notes: SOLVED on attempt 5 (after 4 archived failures). WINNING RECIPE: pure pi0 auto-place, KETCHUP FIRST, then cream cheese, with max_chunks=30 lift_thresh=0.99 — same recipe shape as the cream-cheese auto-place that worked in attempts 2/3/4. KEY INSIGHT from failures 3+4: the TALL ketchup bottle (~10cm) cannot be placed cleanly into a basket that ALREADY HAS the cream cheese box inside — manual descent stalls on the box top (z~0.50) and releasing leaves the bottle perched on the basket back-rim (z~0.55-0.58); even attempt-4 in-basket descent to z=0.694 left the bottle on top of the cream cheese and it rolled out. REVERSING THE ORDER (ketchup first into an EMPTY basket, then the short/flat cream cheese on top/beside) gives pi0's auto-place trajectory enough vertical clearance. Attempt 2's earlier failure of 'pick up the ketchup and place it in the basket' was max_chunks=40 (more time to wander) AND second-after-cream-cheese (less room). With max_chunks=30 AND empty basket, pi0 auto-place succeeds: descend to z=0.55, release, ascend. Total 2 pi0_pick auto-place calls + 1 move_to home — 3 commands. Cream cheese auto-place fired libero_terminated:true in 12 chunks. PROVEN LEVERS reinforced: (a) pi0 auto-place with 'pick up the {X} and place it in the basket', max_chunks=30, lift_thresh=0.99, gripper_closed_thresh=0.06 is the canonical 2-items->basket pattern; (b) place TALL items FIRST when stacking into a small container; (c) NEVER try to manually descend a tall bottle past a flat box in a basket — pi0 auto-place handles the rim clearance better than scripted moves.
